title: The ERDA Book â€“ Democratic Evolution: Strategy, Ethics and the Future of Europe
description:
Release 2.5.0 â€“ Democratic Knowledge

Released: 12 May 2026
Channel: stable release

Release 2.5.0 deepens the normative foundations of the ERDA Book. At its centre is Democratic Knowledge, a seven-principle reference document for democratic agency: power, dealing with non-democracy-willing states, peace and security, truth and deception, democracy itself, technology and AI, and the inner condition of a democratic society.

The release also integrates conceptual papers and implementation material into the book: a guiding vision for raising children in freedom and responsibility (Â§11.3.7), a strategic reflection on the Mosaic Principle and democratic DSN resilience (Â§5.8.4), the Anti-Game-Over Principle as Appendix P.1, CIVITAS Public as Appendix P.2, and a deepened Chapter 6 on CIVITAS as Europe's democratic digital infrastructure. It also adds Appendix M as a measurable book-project yardstick for release criteria, source standards and open project decisions.

Highlights

  • Democratic Knowledge reference material in German and English.
  • New chapter section Â§11.3.7 on child-rearing in freedom and responsibility.
  • New chapter section Â§5.8.4 on the Mosaic Principle and democratic DSN resilience.
  • New Appendix M: Measure â€“ measurable book-project decisions and release criteria.
  • New Appendix P.1: Paper â€“ Childhood, Adulthood, and the Anti-Game-Over Principle.
  • New Appendix P.2: Paper â€“ CIVITAS Public: Building a European Digital Agora.
  • Deepened Chapter 6: CIVITAS as operational democratic digital infrastructure.
  • Worker-roles ethics-before-strategy pass across the ethical foundations and strategic security chapters.
  • Normalized DE/EN content frontmatter and translation mapping metadata.
  • Publish artefacts rebuilt with gitbook_worker 2.9.2; quality gate passed with warnings and no blocked or fail findings.

Release notes: see release-docs/v2.5.0/ in the repository. Texts, graphics and diagrams are licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0; code and build scripts under MIT; own fonts under CC BY 4.0 or MIT; emojis use Twemoji under CC BY 4.0. The canonical attribution register is ATTRIBUTION.md.
